title: woodsia alpina var. glabella
intro: Woodsia alpina var. glabella is a rare and endemic fern species, specifically a type of spleenwort that is native to the Himalayan region. This plant has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, particularly for its anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial effects. The health benefits of Woodsia alpina var. glabella include its potential to treat respiratory issues, such as bronchitis and asthma, as well as its ability to lower blood pressure and improve cardiovascular health. Woodsia alpina var. glabella is often prepared as a tea or tincture, and is also used in traditional herbal medicine as a poultice or topical application, although more research is needed to confirm its effectiveness and safety.
